title: Set up MySQL database and users table
body:
## Description
Create the MySQL database and the `users` table per `docs/database-schema.md`. This is the foundation for authentication.

## Tasks
- Create a `schema.sql` file in the repo root or `backend/migrations/`
- Define the `users` table with columns: `id`, `email`, `password_hash`, `name`, `created_at`
- Verify the schema loads cleanly into a fresh MySQL instance
- Document the connection string format in `CLAUDE.md`

## Acceptance Criteria
- A SQL file exists in the repo that creates the `users` table
- Running it against a fresh database succeeds
- `email` is unique, `password_hash` and `name` are NOT NULL

title: Implement signup endpoint (POST /api/auth/signup)
body:
## Description
Create the signup endpoint that allows new users to register.

## Tasks
- Validate email format and password minimum length
- Hash password with bcrypt before storing
- Insert new user into `users` table
- Return user info (without password) and an auth token
- Reject duplicate emails with 409 Conflict

## Acceptance Criteria
- Can create a new account via curl/Postman
- Second signup with same email returns 409
- Password is stored as bcrypt hash, never plaintext

title: Implement login endpoint (POST /api/auth/login)
body:
## Description
Create the login endpoint that authenticates existing users.

## Tasks
- Look up user by email
- Verify password hash with bcrypt
- Return user info and an auth token on success
- Return 401 on bad credentials (don't leak whether email exists)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Can log in with valid credentials and receive a token
- Invalid password returns 401
- Non-existent email returns 401 (same response as wrong password)

title: Implement auth middleware and GET /api/auth/me
body:
## Description
Create authentication middleware that validates tokens on protected routes, plus a `/me` endpoint for the frontend to verify session validity.

## Tasks
- Middleware extracts and validates the auth token
- Attach authenticated user info to request context
- `GET /api/auth/me` returns the current user
- Reject requests without/with invalid tokens (401)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Hitting `/api/auth/me` without a token returns 401
- With a valid token, returns the user's info
- Middleware can be applied to other routes and works correctly

title: Implement logout endpoint (POST /api/auth/logout)
body:
## Description
Create the logout endpoint that invalidates the session.

## Tasks
- Invalidate the session/token (depends on auth strategy)
- Return success response

## Acceptance Criteria
- After calling logout, the same token can no longer access protected endpoints
- Returns 200 even if token is already invalid (idempotent)

title: Create section tables and rolebooks table
body:
## Description
Create all six remaining tables per `docs/database-schema.md`: `rolebooks`, `contacts`, `projects`, `software`, `recurring_tasks`, `notes`.

## Tasks
- Add table definitions to `schema.sql`
- Configure `ON DELETE CASCADE` foreign keys
- ENUM values match the schema (project status, recurring task cadence)
- All tables have `created_at` and `updated_at` timestamps

## Acceptance Criteria
- Running the SQL file against a fresh database creates all tables
- Foreign keys are correctly configured
- ENUMs accept only the documented values

title: Implement Rolebook endpoints (GET, POST, PUT /api/rolebook)
body:
## Description
Create the endpoints that manage the Rolebook container itself.

## Tasks
- `GET /api/rolebook` returns the user's Rolebook or 404
- `POST /api/rolebook` creates a Rolebook for the user (rejects with 409 if one already exists)
- `PUT /api/rolebook` updates the role title
- All endpoints require authentication

## Acceptance Criteria
- Full lifecycle works via curl: GET returns 404, POST creates, GET returns the rolebook, PUT updates title
- Second POST returns 409 (one rolebook per user)

title: Backend: Contacts CRUD
body:
## Description
Implement all four CRUD endpoints for the `contacts` section.

## Tasks
- `GET /api/contacts` lists all contacts in the user's Rolebook
- `POST /api/contacts` creates a contact
- `PUT /api/contacts/:id` updates a contact (verify ownership)
- `DELETE /api/contacts/:id` deletes a contact (verify ownership)
- All filtered by the authenticated user's Rolebook
- Validate required fields

## Acceptance Criteria
- Full CRUD works via curl
- Cannot access another user's contacts (returns 404)
- Required fields are validated

title: Backend: Projects CRUD
body:
## Description
Implement all four CRUD endpoints for the `projects` section.

## Tasks
- `GET /api/projects` with optional `?status=` query param
- `POST /api/projects` creates a project
- `PUT /api/projects/:id` updates a project
- `DELETE /api/projects/:id` deletes a project
- Validate ENUM value for status

## Acceptance Criteria
- Full CRUD works via curl
- Status filter works correctly
- Invalid status values are rejected

title: Backend: Software CRUD
body:
## Description
Implement all four CRUD endpoints for the `software` section.

## Tasks
- `GET /api/software` lists all software entries
- `POST /api/software` creates an entry
- `PUT /api/software/:id` updates an entry
- `DELETE /api/software/:id` deletes an entry

## Acceptance Criteria
- Full CRUD works via curl
- Ownership scoping is enforced

title: Backend: Recurring Tasks CRUD
body:
## Description
Implement all four CRUD endpoints for the `recurring_tasks` section.

## Tasks
- `GET /api/recurring-tasks` lists all tasks
- `POST /api/recurring-tasks` creates a task
- `PUT /api/recurring-tasks/:id` updates a task
- `DELETE /api/recurring-tasks/:id` deletes a task
- Validate ENUM value for cadence

## Acceptance Criteria
- Full CRUD works via curl
- Invalid cadence values are rejected

title: Backend: Notes CRUD
body:
## Description
Implement all four CRUD endpoints for the `notes` section.

## Tasks
- `GET /api/notes` lists all notes
- `POST /api/notes` creates a note
- `PUT /api/notes/:id` updates a note
- `DELETE /api/notes/:id` deletes a note

## Acceptance Criteria
- Full CRUD works via curl
- Ownership scoping is enforced

title: Implement transfer ownership endpoint (POST /api/rolebook/transfer)
body:
## Description
Create the endpoint that transfers Rolebook ownership to another user.

## Tasks
- Authenticated, only callable by the current owner
- Look up target user by email
- Return 404 with `{error: 'no_account'}` if email not registered
- Update `rolebooks.owner_id` to new owner's ID
- Return success message

## Acceptance Criteria
- Full transfer flow works via curl
- Transfer to non-registered email returns 404 with the documented error format
- After transfer, original owner can no longer access the Rolebook

title: Add gofpdf dependency and PDF generator scaffolding
body:
## Description
Set up the PDF generation library and create the scaffolding for the PDF generator.

## Tasks
- Add `gofpdf` (or equivalent) to `go.mod`
- Create a `pdf` package with a `GeneratePDF(rolebook *Rolebook) ([]byte, error)` function stub
- Verify it can produce a one-page Hello World PDF

## Acceptance Criteria
- A unit test or simple endpoint produces a valid PDF file
- The PDF opens correctly in standard viewers

## Note
If this issue takes more than 2 hours due to library issues, fall back to the browser-print approach (replace #14-16 with a single 'add print-friendly CSS' issue).

title: Implement PDF formatting for all five sections
body:
## Description
Flesh out the PDF generator to render a complete Rolebook with all sections.

## Tasks
- Header with role title and export date
- One section block per section with heading and entry list
- Reasonable typography (no fancy styling, but readable)
- Empty sections are skipped or note 'No entries yet'

## Acceptance Criteria
- A Rolebook with entries in all sections renders cleanly to PDF
- An empty Rolebook still produces a valid PDF
- Long entries don't break layout

title: Implement PDF export endpoint (GET /api/rolebook/export)
body:
## Description
Create the endpoint that exports the user's Rolebook as a PDF download.

## Tasks
- Authenticated, scoped to user's Rolebook
- Reads all five section tables and the rolebook record
- Calls the PDF generator
- Returns `application/pdf` with `Content-Disposition: attachment` for download

## Acceptance Criteria
- Hitting the endpoint downloads a valid PDF in the browser
- Filename is reasonable (e.g., `rolebook-{role-title}-{date}.pdf`)

title: Build Landing page UI
body:
## Description
Build the public landing page at `/`.

## Tasks
- Brief explanation of what Rolebook is
- Log In button (linking to `/login`)
- Sign Up link (linking to `/signup`)
- Logged-in users redirect to `/dashboard`

## Acceptance Criteria
- Unauthenticated visitors see the landing page
- Authenticated users get redirected to dashboard

title: Build Sign Up page UI
body:
## Description
Build the signup form at `/signup`.

## Tasks
- Form with email, password, name fields
- Calls `/api/auth/signup`
- Stores the returned token client-side
- Redirects to `/onboarding` on success
- Displays validation and server errors

## Acceptance Criteria
- End-to-end signup works in the browser
- Errors are displayed inline (e.g., 'Email already registered')
- After success, user lands on onboarding

title: Build Log In page UI
body:
## Description
Build the login form at `/login`.

## Tasks
- Form with email and password fields
- Calls `/api/auth/login`
- Stores the returned token client-side
- Redirects to `/dashboard` on success (or `/onboarding` if no Rolebook yet)
- Displays errors

## Acceptance Criteria
- End-to-end login works in the browser
- Invalid credentials show an inline error
- New users without a Rolebook are routed to onboarding

title: Implement protected-route logic in the React app
body:
## Description
Protect routes that require authentication and verify token validity on app load.

## Tasks
- Routes under `/dashboard` and `/settings` require authentication
- Unauthenticated users redirect to `/login`
- On app load, hit `/api/auth/me` to verify token is still valid
- If token invalid, clear it and redirect to login

## Acceptance Criteria
- Visiting a protected route while logged out redirects to `/login`
- Visiting while logged in loads the page
- Expired tokens are detected and the user is logged out gracefully

title: Build Onboarding page UI
body:
## Description
Build the first-time setup page at `/onboarding`.

## Tasks
- Single form with role title field
- Calls `POST /api/rolebook`
- Redirects to `/dashboard/contacts` on success
- Logged-in users with an existing Rolebook redirect to dashboard

## Acceptance Criteria
- New user sees onboarding once and never again
- Invalid input (empty title) is rejected with inline error
- After success, user lands on the dashboard

title: Build Dashboard shell with section tabs
body:
## Description
Build the dashboard layout and tab navigation. This is the foundation for all section tabs.

## Tasks
- Layout with navigation header (logo, role title, settings menu)
- Tab bar with Contacts | Projects | Software | Recurring | Notes
- Routes `/dashboard/:section` render the active tab
- `/dashboard` redirects to `/dashboard/contacts`
- Empty state placeholder for each section (will be replaced by section UIs)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Can navigate between tabs, URL updates correctly
- Header is stable across all tabs
- Active tab is visually highlighted

title: Frontend: Contacts tab UI
body:
## Description
Build the Contacts tab content with full add/edit/delete UI.

## Tasks
- List view with entry cards showing name, role, communication preferences
- 'Add Contact' button opens form modal
- Edit and delete actions on each card
- Form fields: name, role, relationship_notes, communication_preferences, watch_out_for
- Empty state when no contacts exist

## Acceptance Criteria
- Can add, edit, delete contacts in the browser
- Changes persist after page reload
- Form validation matches backend (required fields)

title: Frontend: Projects tab UI
body:
## Description
Build the Projects tab content with full CRUD UI.

## Tasks
- List view with entry cards (showing status badge prominently)
- Add/edit form with title, status dropdown, deadline date picker, notes
- Empty state when no projects exist
- Optional: filter by status

## Acceptance Criteria
- Can add, edit, delete projects in the browser
- Status is visually distinct (active/on hold/done)
- Date picker produces valid dates

title: Frontend: Software tab UI
body:
## Description
Build the Software tab content with full CRUD UI.

## Tasks
- List view with entry cards
- Add/edit form with name, purpose, credentials_location, notes
- Help text near `credentials_location` field reminding users to store the pointer, not the credential
- Empty state when no software exists

## Acceptance Criteria
- Can add, edit, delete software entries in the browser
- Help text about credentials is visible and clear

title: Frontend: Recurring Tasks tab UI
body:
## Description
Build the Recurring Tasks tab content with full CRUD UI.

## Tasks
- List view with entry cards (showing cadence badge)
- Add/edit form with name, cadence dropdown, notes
- Empty state when no recurring tasks exist

## Acceptance Criteria
- Can add, edit, delete recurring tasks in the browser
- Cadence values match the four documented options

title: Frontend: Notes tab UI
body:
## Description
Build the Notes tab content with full CRUD UI.

## Tasks
- List view with entry cards (title prominent, body preview)
- Add/edit form with title and body
- Click a card to expand and see the full body
- Empty state when no notes exist

## Acceptance Criteria
- Can add, edit, delete notes in the browser
- Long notes are previewed cleanly without breaking the layout
- Full body is accessible on click

title: Add PDF export button to Dashboard
body:
## Description
Wire up the PDF export feature in the UI.

## Tasks
- 'Export to PDF' button in the navigation header or settings menu
- Triggers download via `GET /api/rolebook/export`
- Show loading state during generation

## Acceptance Criteria
- Clicking the button downloads a PDF
- Button is disabled or shows loading during the request

title: Build Settings page UI
body:
## Description
Build the settings page at `/settings`.

## Tasks
- Display current role title (editable, calls `PUT /api/rolebook`)
- Display user info (name, email)
- Log out button
- 'Transfer my Rolebook' button linking to `/settings/transfer`
- (Optional) Change password section

## Acceptance Criteria
- Settings page renders with all the above
- Role title edit works and persists

title: Build Transfer Ownership page UI
body:
## Description
Build the transfer ownership flow at `/settings/transfer`.

## Tasks
- Clear warning that transfer is permanent
- Email input field
- 'Confirm transfer' button with secondary confirmation modal
- Calls `POST /api/rolebook/transfer`
- On success: log out and redirect to landing
- On error (email not registered): show inline error

## Acceptance Criteria
- Full transfer flow works in the browser
- Cannot accidentally transfer (requires explicit confirmation)
- 'no_account' error displays a helpful message
